---
title: "Example post: the Bloch sphere"
description: "Template demonstrating the math, figure, citation, and code-snippet patterns."
date: "2026-05-23"
categories: [example, template]
image: ../../figures/bloch_sphere.svg
---

Placeholder post. Replace the prose; keep the patterns below as a
reference for how to write equations, figures, citations, and code
snippets in this site.

## Inline and display math {#sec-math}

Inline math: $|\psi\rangle = \alpha\,|0\rangle + \beta\,|1\rangle$,
with $|\alpha|^2 + |\beta|^2 = 1$.

A numbered display equation, labeled with `{#eq-state}`:

$$
|\psi(\theta, \phi)\rangle \;=\; \cos\!\tfrac{\theta}{2}\,|0\rangle
  \;+\; e^{i\phi}\sin\!\tfrac{\theta}{2}\,|1\rangle.
$$ {#eq-state}

Cross-reference it inline as @eq-state, or a section as @sec-math.

## A figure produced by `code/`

Figures are written by a script in `code/` and embedded as a saved SVG —
nothing executes at build time.

![Placeholder caption. Generated by [`code/bloch_sphere.py`](../../code/bloch_sphere.py).](../../figures/bloch_sphere.svg){#fig-bloch width=70%}

Cross-reference it as @fig-bloch.

## An inert code snippet

The block below is shown for reading only; the build does not run it.

```python
def bloch_vector(theta: float, phi: float) -> np.ndarray:
    return np.array([
        np.sin(theta) * np.cos(phi),
        np.sin(theta) * np.sin(phi),
        np.cos(theta),
    ])
```

## A citation

Cite an entry from `references.bib` with `@bibkey`, e.g. @nielsen_chuang_2010.
A parenthetical citation uses `[@bibkey]` [@bloch_1946].

## References {.unnumbered}

::: {#refs}
:::
